prompt:
A local library uses a software system to manage patron accounts. Each patron has a library card that records their name, the number of books currently checked out, the total fines owed, and whether they hold a premium membership. You will write the complete LibraryCard class to model these accounts.

The following describes the required class members:

LibraryCard(String name, boolean isPremium) — Constructs a new LibraryCard. Sets patronName to name, premium to isPremium; initializes booksCheckedOut to 0 and finesOwed to 0.0.
void checkOutBook() — Increments booksCheckedOut by 1.
void addFine(double amount) — Adds amount to the running total of finesOwed.
String getAccountSummary() — Returns a formatted multi-line String containing all account fields.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the class header for LibraryCard. Declare all required private instance variables: patronName (String), booksCheckedOut (int), finesOwed (double), and premium (boolean). Write the constructor LibraryCard(String name, boolean isPremium) that initializes every instance variable. The fields booksCheckedOut and finesOwed must be set to 0 and 0.0 respectively; patronName and premium are set from the parameters.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt:
Write the three instance methods of LibraryCard.
void checkOutBook() increments booksCheckedOut by 1.
void addFine(double amount) accumulates amount onto finesOwed (do not overwrite the existing total).
String getAccountSummary() returns a String in the following format, with each item on its own line:
Name: <patronName>
Books Checked Out: <booksCheckedOut>
Fines Owed: <finesOwed>
Membership: Premium Member   (when premium is true)
Membership: Standard Member  (when premium is false)

rubric.criterion: Class is declared public class LibraryCard with no extends, implements, or interface keywords.
All four fields declared private with correct types: patronName (String), booksCheckedOut (int), finesOwed (double), premium (boolean). No field is public or static.
Constructor is named LibraryCard and accepts exactly (String name, boolean isPremium) as parameters in that order.
All four fields initialized: patronName assigned from name, booksCheckedOut set to 0, finesOwed set to 0.0, premium assigned from isPremium. All four assignments present.
Declared void checkOutBook() with no parameters; body increments booksCheckedOut by exactly 1 (e.g., booksCheckedOut++ or booksCheckedOut += 1).
Declared void addFine(double amount); body accumulates using +=: finesOwed += amount. Using = instead of += does not earn this point.

Declared String getAccountSummary(); returns a String containing all four fields separated by \n characters; selects Premium Member when premium is true and Standard Member when false; no System.out.print calls inside the method.

test_cases.description: standard membership with two checkouts and a cumulative fine
test_cases.expected_output:
Name: Alice
Books Checked Out: 2
Fines Owed: 2.5
Membership: Standard Member

test_cases.description: premium membership with one checkout and initial zero balance
test_cases.expected_output:
Name: Bob
Books Checked Out: 1
Fines Owed: 0.0
Membership: Premium Member

test_cases.description: fines accumulated across two addFine calls with no books checked out
test_cases.expected_output:
Name: Carol
Books Checked Out: 0
Fines Owed: 4.5
Membership: Standard Member

prompt:
A fitness app tracks a runner's weekly training progress. Each user has a running log that records their name, their weekly mileage goal, the total miles run so far this week, and how many individual runs they have logged. You will write the complete RunningLog class to model this data.

The following describes the required class members:

RunningLog(String name, double weeklyGoal) — Constructs a RunningLog with a specific weekly goal. Sets runnerName to name, weeklyGoalMiles to weeklyGoal; initializes milesRunThisWeek to 0.0 and totalRuns to 0.
RunningLog(String name) — Constructs a RunningLog with no weekly goal. Sets runnerName to name; initializes weeklyGoalMiles to 0.0, milesRunThisWeek to 0.0, and totalRuns to 0.
void logRun(double miles) — Records a run of the given distance and increments the run count.
boolean onPace() — Returns true if the runner has already met or exceeded the weekly mileage goal.
String getSummary() — Returns a formatted multi-line String with the runner's name, total runs, and miles run this week.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt:
Write the class header for RunningLog. Declare all required private instance variables: runnerName (String), weeklyGoalMiles (double), milesRunThisWeek (double), and totalRuns (int). Write both constructors:
- RunningLog(String name, double weeklyGoal): sets runnerName from name, weeklyGoalMiles from weeklyGoal; sets milesRunThisWeek to 0.0 and totalRuns to 0.
- RunningLog(String name): sets runnerName from name; explicitly defaults weeklyGoalMiles to 0.0, milesRunThisWeek to 0.0, and totalRuns to 0.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt:
Write the three instance methods of RunningLog.
void logRun(double miles) adds miles to milesRunThisWeek (cumulative) and increments totalRuns by 1.
boolean onPace() returns true if milesRunThisWeek is greater than or equal to weeklyGoalMiles, and false otherwise. The method must return a boolean; it must not call System.out.print.
String getSummary() returns a String in the following format, with each item on its own line:
Runner: <runnerName>
Total Runs: <totalRuns>
Miles This Week: <milesRunThisWeek>

rubric.criterion: Class declared as public class RunningLog with no extends, implements, or interface keywords.
All four fields declared private with correct types: runnerName (String), weeklyGoalMiles (double), milesRunThisWeek (double), totalRuns (int). No field is public or static.
Both constructors are named RunningLog; the two-parameter constructor accepts (String name, double weeklyGoal) and the one-parameter constructor accepts (String name) only, in the correct order.

In the two-parameter constructor, all four fields are assigned (weeklyGoalMiles from weeklyGoal, others as defaults or parameters). In the one-parameter constructor, weeklyGoalMiles is explicitly set to 0.0 (not left uninitialized), along with milesRunThisWeek and totalRuns.
Declared void logRun(double miles); body uses += to accumulate: milesRunThisWeek += miles; and increments totalRuns by 1. Both updates must be present.
Declared boolean onPace() with no parameters; returns the result of milesRunThisWeek >= weeklyGoalMiles (or logically equivalent expression). No System.out.print calls inside the method.

Declared String getSummary(); returns a String containing runnerName, totalRuns, and milesRunThisWeek on separate lines using \n, with the labels Runner:, Total Runs:, and Miles This Week: exactly. No System.out.print calls inside the method.

test_cases.description: runner not yet on pace after two runs
test_cases.expected_output:
false
Runner: Dana
Total Runs: 2
Miles This Week: 11.5

test_cases.description: runner exactly meets the weekly goal boundary
no-goal constructor defaults weeklyGoalMiles to 0.0 so any logged run is on pace
test_cases.expected_output:
true
Runner: Fiona
Total Runs: 1
Miles This Week: 3.0

prompt:
A movie theater loyalty program issues digital tickets that double as reward cards. Each ticket stores the holder's name, the movie title, the seat tier (premium or standard), and the holder's current reward point balance. Points can be earned by purchasing tickets and redeemed for discounts, but only when the current balance is sufficient. You will write the complete MovieTicket class to model this system.

The following describes the required class members:

MovieTicket(String holder, String movie, boolean isPremium) — Constructs a MovieTicket. Sets holderName to holder, movieTitle to movie, premiumSeat to isPremium; initializes rewardPoints to 0.
void addPoints(int points) — Adds points to the reward point total.
void redeemPoints(int points) — Subtracts points from the reward total only if the current balance is greater than or equal to points; otherwise does nothing.
String getTicketInfo() — Returns a formatted multi-line String containing all four fields, with the seat tier displayed as Premium or Standard.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the class header for MovieTicket. Declare all required private instance variables: holderName (String), movieTitle (String), premiumSeat (boolean), and rewardPoints (int). Write the constructor MovieTicket(String holder, String movie, boolean isPremium) that initializes every instance variable. The field rewardPoints must be set to 0; the remaining fields are set from the constructor parameters.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt:
Write the three instance methods of MovieTicket.
void addPoints(int points) adds points to rewardPoints (cumulative total; do not overwrite).
void redeemPoints(int points) subtracts points from rewardPoints only when rewardPoints >= points; if the current balance is insufficient the method must leave rewardPoints unchanged.
String getTicketInfo() returns a String in the following format, with each item on its own line:
Holder: <holderName>
Movie: <movieTitle>
Seat: Premium   (when premiumSeat is true)
Seat: Standard  (when premiumSeat is false)
Reward Points: <rewardPoints>

rubric.criterion: Class declared as public class MovieTicket with no extends, implements, or interface keywords.
All four fields declared private with correct types: holderName (String), movieTitle (String), premiumSeat (boolean), rewardPoints (int). No field is public or static.
Constructor is named MovieTicket and accepts exactly (String holder, String movie, boolean isPremium) as parameters in that order.
All four fields initialized: holderName from holder, movieTitle from movie, premiumSeat from isPremium, and rewardPoints explicitly set to 0.
Declared void addPoints(int points); body uses += to accumulate: rewardPoints += points. Using = instead of += does not earn this point.

Declared void redeemPoints(int points); body contains an if statement that checks rewardPoints >= points before subtracting; rewardPoints -= points executes only when the condition is true; method does nothing when balance is insufficient.

Declared String getTicketInfo(); returns a String with holderName, movieTitle, conditional seat label (Premium or Standard based on premiumSeat), and rewardPoints each separated by \n; no System.out.print calls inside the method.

test_cases.description: standard seat with two addPoints calls then summary
test_cases.expected_output:
Holder: Grace
Movie: Starfall
Seat: Standard
Reward Points: 80

test_cases.description: premium seat with a successful redemption
test_cases.expected_output:
Holder: Hank
Movie: Luna Rising
Seat: Premium
Reward Points: 60

test_cases.description: failed redemption leaves balance unchanged when points are insufficient
test_cases.expected_output:
Holder: Iris
Movie: Orbit
Seat: Premium
Reward Points: 20

prompt:
A fitness gym uses software to track member progress toward monthly workout goals. You will write a complete Java class called GymMembership.

The GymMembership class has four private instance variables: memberName (String), monthlyGoal (int), minutesLogged (int), and classesAttended (int).

GymMembership has two constructors:
- GymMembership(String memberName, int monthlyGoal): initializes all fields; minutesLogged and classesAttended each start at 0.
- GymMembership(String memberName): sets monthlyGoal to a default of 600 minutes; minutesLogged and classesAttended each start at 0.

GymMembership has two methods:
- void logWorkout(int minutes): adds minutes to minutesLogged and increments classesAttended by 1.
- String getMemberStatus(): returns a formatted String containing the member name, monthly goal, total minutes logged, classes attended, and a status line. The status reads "On Track" if minutesLogged >= monthlyGoal, or "Below Goal" otherwise.

Sample: given GymMembership g = new GymMembership("Taylor", 300) followed by g.logWorkout(150) and g.logWorkout(200), g.getMemberStatus() returns the following (each item on its own line):
Member: Taylor
Monthly Goal: 300 min
Logged: 350 min
Classes: 2
Status: On Track

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete GymMembership class. Your class must include all private instance variables, both constructors, the logWorkout method, and the getMemberStatus method. No class skeleton is provided.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public with the exact name GymMembership (public class GymMembership); no extends, implements, or abstract keywords present
All four fields declared private with correct types: memberName (String), monthlyGoal (int), minutesLogged (int), classesAttended (int); no field is public or static
Both constructors are named GymMembership with no return type; first accepts (String, int) parameters; second accepts (String) only

Two-parameter constructor assigns memberName and monthlyGoal from params and explicitly sets minutesLogged and classesAttended to 0; one-parameter constructor sets monthlyGoal to 600 and both counters to 0
Method declared with return type void, named exactly logWorkout, and exactly one int parameter
minutesLogged is increased by the parameter value using += (not overwritten with =); classesAttended is incremented by 1 in the same method body

Method declares return type String and returns (does not print) a multi-line String using \n separators for all five labeled lines; status branch produces "On Track" when minutesLogged >= monthlyGoal and "Below Goal" otherwise

test_cases.description: Two-parameter constructor; two logWorkout calls accumulate past the monthly goal
test_cases.expected_output:
Member: Taylor
Monthly Goal: 300 min
Logged: 350 min
Classes: 2
Status: On Track

test_cases.description: Two-parameter constructor; single logWorkout call leaves total below the monthly goal
test_cases.expected_output:
Member: Jordan
Monthly Goal: 500 min
Logged: 100 min
Classes: 1
Status: Below Goal

test_cases.description: One-parameter constructor applies default goal of 600; no workouts logged yet
test_cases.setup_java:
GymMembership g = new GymMembership("Sam");
System.out.println(g.getMemberStatus());
test_cases.expected_output:
Member: Sam
Monthly Goal: 600 min
Logged: 0 min
Classes: 0
Status: Below Goal

prompt:
A nonprofit organization awards certification to volunteers who complete a required number of service hours. You will write a complete Java class called VolunteerRecord.

The VolunteerRecord class has four private instance variables: volunteerName (String), organization (String), hoursRequired (int), and hoursCompleted (int).

VolunteerRecord has two constructors:
- VolunteerRecord(String volunteerName, String organization, int hoursRequired): initializes all fields; hoursCompleted starts at 0.
- VolunteerRecord(String volunteerName, String organization): sets hoursRequired to a default of 100; hoursCompleted starts at 0.

VolunteerRecord has two methods:
- void logHours(int hours): adds hours to hoursCompleted.
- String getCertificationStatus(): returns a formatted String with the volunteer name, organization, hours completed, hours required, and a status line. The status reads "Certified" if hoursCompleted >= hoursRequired, or "Not Yet Certified" otherwise.

Sample: given VolunteerRecord v = new VolunteerRecord("Maya", "Food Bank", 50) followed by v.logHours(30) and v.logHours(25), v.getCertificationStatus() returns the following (each item on its own line):
Volunteer: Maya
Org: Food Bank
Completed: 55 hrs
Required: 50 hrs
Status: Certified

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete VolunteerRecord class. Your class must include all private instance variables, both constructors, the logHours method, and the getCertificationStatus method. No class skeleton is provided.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public with the exact name VolunteerRecord (public class VolunteerRecord); no extends, implements, or abstract keywords present
All four fields declared private with correct types: volunteerName (String), organization (String), hoursRequired (int), hoursCompleted (int); no field is public or static
Both constructors are named VolunteerRecord with no return type; first accepts (String, String, int) parameters; second accepts (String, String) only

Three-parameter constructor assigns volunteerName, organization, and hoursRequired from params and explicitly sets hoursCompleted to 0; two-parameter constructor sets hoursRequired to 100 and hoursCompleted to 0
Method declared with return type void, named exactly logHours, and exactly one int parameter
hoursCompleted is increased by the parameter value using += (accumulation, not overwrite with =); no other field is modified

Method declares return type String and returns (does not print) a multi-line String using \n separators for all five labeled lines; status branch produces "Certified" when hoursCompleted >= hoursRequired and "Not Yet Certified" otherwise

test_cases.description: Three-parameter constructor; two logHours calls push total above the required hours
test_cases.expected_output:
Volunteer: Maya
Org: Food Bank
Completed: 55 hrs
Required: 50 hrs
Status: Certified

test_cases.description: Three-parameter constructor; single logHours call leaves total short of required hours
test_cases.expected_output:
Volunteer: Ethan
Org: Animal Shelter
Completed: 75 hrs
Required: 200 hrs
Status: Not Yet Certified

test_cases.description: Two-parameter constructor applies default 100-hour requirement; no hours logged yet
test_cases.setup_java:
VolunteerRecord v = new VolunteerRecord("Priya", "Library");
System.out.println(v.getCertificationStatus());
test_cases.expected_output:
Volunteer: Priya
Org: Library
Completed: 0 hrs
Required: 100 hrs
Status: Not Yet Certified

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete LibraryBook class, including all private instance variables, both constructors, the checkOut method, and the getInfo method, exactly as specified above.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public class LibraryBook with no extends/implements clause.
Declares exactly four private instance fields with correct types: String title, String author, int timesCheckedOut, boolean isCheckedOut (or isomorphic names). No field is public or static.
Both constructor signatures are present and correct: public LibraryBook(String, String, int) and public LibraryBook(String, String). Constructor name matches class name exactly.
Each constructor assigns every instance field. The two-parameter constructor explicitly defaults timesCheckedOut to 0. Both constructors set isCheckedOut to false.
checkOut method has signature public void checkOut() with no parameters and void return type.

checkOut conditionally tests isCheckedOut: only when the book is not currently checked out does it set isCheckedOut to true AND increment timesCheckedOut by 1. Already-checked-out books are unchanged.

getInfo returns (does not print) a String concatenating title, ' by ', author, status string with parentheses, and ', checkouts: ' followed by timesCheckedOut. Status string branches correctly on isCheckedOut between 'currently checked out' and 'available'.

test_cases.description: three-arg constructor, no checkOut called
test_cases.setup_java:
LibraryBook b = new LibraryBook("Dune", "Herbert", 5);
System.out.println(b.getInfo());

test_cases.description: two-arg constructor; second checkOut must not double-increment
three-arg constructor, then one checkOut increments lifetime count
test_cases.expected_output: Hamlet by Shakespeare (currently checked out), checkouts: 13

prompt:
A personal-finance app models each savings goal with a SavingsGoal object. Each goal records its name, the dollar target, the dollar amount currently saved, and a priority level encoded as an integer (1 = high, 2 = medium, anything else = low). Write the complete SavingsGoal class so that the calls in the table below behave as described.

Method / Constructor signatures:
  public SavingsGoal(String goalName, double targetAmount, double currentAmount, int priority)
      - Constructs a goal initialized from all four parameters.
  public SavingsGoal(String goalName, double targetAmount, int priority)
      - Constructs a brand-new goal with no money saved yet. The currentAmount field must default to 0.0.
  public void deposit(double amount)
      - Adds amount to the current saved amount (accumulates; do not overwrite).
  public String getStatus()
      - Returns a String of the form: <goalName>: $<currentAmount> of $<targetAmount> [<label>]
        where <label> is exactly:
          'high priority' when priority equals 1,
          'medium priority' when priority equals 2,
          'low priority' for any other priority value.

All instance variables must be private. Do NOT print anything from inside getStatus.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete SavingsGoal class, including all private instance variables, both overloaded constructors, the deposit mutator, and the getStatus accessor, exactly as specified above.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public class SavingsGoal with no extends/implements clause.
Declares exactly four private instance fields with correct types: String goalName, double targetAmount, double currentAmount, int priority. No field is public or static.

Both overloaded constructor signatures are present and correct: public SavingsGoal(String, double, double, int) and public SavingsGoal(String, double, int). Constructor name matches class name exactly.

Each constructor assigns every instance field. The three-parameter constructor explicitly defaults currentAmount to 0.0. The four-parameter constructor copies all four parameters into the matching fields.
deposit method has signature public void deposit(double amount) with one double parameter and void return type.
deposit uses += (or equivalent currentAmount = currentAmount + amount) to accumulate, NOT plain assignment. After multiple deposits the field reflects the total.

rubric.point_label: getStatus accessor returns correctly branched String
rubric.criterion: getStatus returns (does not print) a String concatenating goalName + ': $' + currentAmount + ' of $' + targetAmount + ' [' + label + ']'. The label is selected by branching on priority: 1 yields 'high priority', 2 yields 'medium priority', any other value yields 'low priority'.

test_cases.description: three-arg constructor defaults currentAmount to 0.0; one deposit; high priority
four-arg constructor; multiple deposits accumulate; medium priority
three-arg constructor, no deposits, priority 3 falls through to low
test_cases.setup_java:
SavingsGoal g = new SavingsGoal("Gift", 200.0, 3);
System.out.println(g.getStatus());

prompt:
A small public library tracks the books it loans out. You will write a complete class LibraryBook that stores information about a single book title and how many physical copies of that title are currently on the shelf.

A LibraryBook keeps track of the following information:
- the title of the book (a String)
- the author of the book (a String)
- the total number of copies the library owns (an int)
- the number of copies currently available to be checked out (an int)

The class must support the methods shown in the table below.

Method / Constructor                              | Description
--------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------
LibraryBook(String t, String a, int copies)       | Initializes title to t, author to a, and sets BOTH totalCopies and copiesAvailable to copies (every copy starts on the shelf).
LibraryBook(String t, String a)                   | Initializes title to t and author to a. Sets totalCopies and copiesAvailable to 0 (the library has none of this title yet).
void checkOut()                                   | Decreases copiesAvailable by 1 (a patron has taken a copy off the shelf). You may assume copiesAvailable > 0 when this is called.
String getStatus()                                | Returns a formatted status line. If copiesAvailable is 0, returns the title, then " by ", then the author, then ": unavailable". Otherwise returns the title, then " by ", then the author, then ": ", then copiesAvailable, then " of ", then totalCopies, then " available".

Write the complete LibraryBook class. Your implementation must include all instance variables, both constructors, and both methods. All instance variables must be private. Do not write a main method.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete LibraryBook class as specified above. Include the class header, all private instance variables, both constructors, the checkOut method, and the getStatus method.

rubric.criterion: Declares the class as public class LibraryBook with the exact specified name.
Declares exactly four private instance fields: a String title, a String author, an int totalCopies, and an int copiesAvailable. None of the fields are public or static.
Both constructors are present with the exact name LibraryBook and parameter lists (String, String, int) and (String, String) in that order. No return types declared.

Three-arg constructor assigns title, author, totalCopies, and copiesAvailable from / to copies. Two-arg constructor assigns title and author from parameters and explicitly defaults totalCopies and copiesAvailable to 0.
Declares public void checkOut() with no parameters and return type void.
Body decreases copiesAvailable by 1 (using -=, --, or copiesAvailable = copiesAvailable - 1). Does not modify any other field.

getStatus is declared to return String, branches on copiesAvailable == 0 to return the unavailable form, otherwise returns the title + " by " + author + ": " + copiesAvailable + " of " + totalCopies + " available". Returns the String — does not print it.

test_cases.description: basic three-arg constructor, fully stocked
test_cases.setup_java:
LibraryBook b = new LibraryBook("Dune", "Herbert", 3);
System.out.println(b.getStatus());

test_cases.description: checkOut twice leaves one copy available
two-arg constructor reports unavailable
test_cases.setup_java:
LibraryBook b = new LibraryBook("Foo", "Bar");
System.out.println(b.getStatus());

prompt:
A bank tracks savings accounts. You will write a complete class SavingsAccount that records information about a single customer's account.

A SavingsAccount keeps track of the following information:
- the account holder's name (a String)
- the account number (an int)
- the annual interest rate as a decimal, e.g. 0.05 for 5% (a double)
- the current account balance in dollars (a double)

The class must support the constructors and methods shown in the table below.

Method / Constructor                                                | Description
--------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------
SavingsAccount(String holder, int num, double rate, double initBal) | Initializes accountHolder to holder, accountNumber to num, interestRate to rate, and balance to initBal.
SavingsAccount(String holder, int num, double rate)                 | Initializes accountHolder, accountNumber, and interestRate from the parameters. Sets balance to 0.0 (a brand new account with no money deposited yet).
void deposit(double amount)                                         | Adds amount to balance. Multiple deposits should accumulate; do not overwrite the previous balance.
String getSummary()                                                 | Returns a one-line summary of the account. The label inside the parentheses is "premium" when interestRate is greater than or equal to 0.05, and "standard" otherwise. Format: "Account #" + accountNumber + " (" + label + "): " + accountHolder + ", balance $" + balance.

Write the complete SavingsAccount class. Your implementation must include all instance variables, both constructors, and both methods. All instance variables must be private. Do not write a main method.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete SavingsAccount class as specified above. Include the class header, all private instance variables, both constructors, the deposit method, and the getSummary method.

rubric.criterion: Declares the class as public class SavingsAccount with the exact specified name.
Declares exactly four private instance fields with correct types: String accountHolder, int accountNumber, double interestRate, double balance. None of the fields are public or static.
Both constructors are present with the exact name SavingsAccount and parameter lists (String, int, double, double) and (String, int, double) in that order. No return types declared.

Four-arg constructor assigns accountHolder, accountNumber, interestRate, and balance from parameters. Three-arg constructor assigns accountHolder, accountNumber, and interestRate from parameters and explicitly defaults balance to 0.0.
Declares public void deposit(double amount) with return type void and a single double parameter.
Body adds amount to balance using += or balance = balance + amount. Multiple calls accumulate; the body does not overwrite balance with amount.

getSummary is declared to return String, branches on interestRate >= 0.05 to choose "premium" vs. "standard", and returns a string of the form "Account #" + accountNumber + " (" + label + "): " + accountHolder + ", balance $" + balance. Returns the String — does not print it.

test_cases.description: four-arg constructor, low rate uses standard label
test_cases.setup_java:
SavingsAccount a = new SavingsAccount("Alice", 1234, 0.03, 500.0);
System.out.println(a.getSummary());

test_cases.description: three-arg constructor defaults balance to 0; deposits accumulate; high rate uses premium label
boundary: rate exactly 0.05 is premium; deposit adds to initial balance

prompt:
A local library tracks books that patrons borrow. You will write a complete BookLoan class to represent one loan record. A BookLoan object stores the title of the book (a String), the patron's name (a String), the number of days the book has been checked out (an int), and the daily late fee in cents once the loan goes overdue (an int). A loan is considered overdue when the number of days checked out is greater than 14.

The class supports the following behavior:

- BookLoan(String title, String patron, int days, int dailyFee) — constructs a BookLoan with all four fields supplied.
- BookLoan(String title, String patron) — constructs a BookLoan with the given title and patron, sets days to 0, and sets dailyFee to 25 cents.
- void extendLoan(int extraDays) — adds extraDays to the running total of days checked out.
- int currentFine() — returns the total fine in cents. If the loan is not overdue (days <= 14), return 0. Otherwise return (days - 14) * dailyFee.
- String summary() — returns a String of the form "<title> borrowed by <patron>: <days> days, fine = <fine>c" where <fine> is the value returned by currentFine().

Write the complete BookLoan class. All instance variables must be private.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete BookLoan class as specified above, including all private instance variables, both constructors, extendLoan, currentFine, and summary.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public class BookLoan
All four required fields (title String, patron String, days int, dailyFee int) declared private with correct types
Constructor BookLoan(String, String, int, int) has correct header and assigns all four fields from parameters
Constructor BookLoan(String, String) has correct header, assigns title and patron from parameters, and defaults days to 0 and dailyFee to 25
extendLoan has return type void, takes one int parameter, and uses += (or equivalent) to add extraDays to the days field rather than overwriting it
currentFine returns int; returns 0 when days <= 14, otherwise returns (days - 14) * dailyFee
summary returns a String exactly of the form "<title> borrowed by <patron>: <days> days, fine = <fine>c" where fine is computed from currentFine; uses return (not System.out.print)

test_cases.description: two-arg constructor, not overdue
test_cases.expected_output: Hamlet borrowed by Ana: 10 days, fine = 0c

test_cases.description: four-arg constructor, overdue with custom fee
test_cases.expected_output:
180
Dune borrowed by Marcus: 20 days, fine = 180c

test_cases.description: default fee after extending past due

prompt:
A coffee shop loyalty program tracks each customer with a CoffeeCard object. Every CoffeeCard stores the customer's name (a String), the customer's tier code (an int, where 0 = standard, 1 = gold, 2 = platinum), the number of points earned so far (an int), and whether auto-renewal is enabled (a boolean).

The class supports the following behavior:

- CoffeeCard(String name, int tier, int points, boolean autoRenew) — constructs a card with all four fields supplied.
- CoffeeCard(String name) — constructs a card for a brand-new customer; sets tier to 0, points to 0, and autoRenew to false.
- void earnPoints(int amount) — adds amount to the running total of points earned.
- void upgradeTier() — if tier is 0, set it to 1; if tier is 1, set it to 2; if tier is already 2, leave it unchanged.
- String tierLabel() — returns "standard" if tier is 0, "gold" if tier is 1, and "platinum" if tier is 2.
- String describe() — returns a String of the form "<name> | <tierLabel> | <points> pts | renew=<autoRenew>" using the value returned by tierLabel().

Write the complete CoffeeCard class. All instance variables must be private.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the complete CoffeeCard class, including private instance variables, both constructors, and all required methods (earnPoints, upgradeTier, tierLabel, describe).

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public class CoffeeCard
All four required fields (name String, tier int, points int, autoRenew boolean) declared private with correct types
CoffeeCard(String, int, int, boolean) has correct header and assigns all four fields from parameters
CoffeeCard(String) has correct header, assigns name from parameter, and defaults tier to 0, points to 0, and autoRenew to false
earnPoints has return type void, one int parameter, and uses += to accumulate points (not =)
upgradeTier promotes 0 -> 1 and 1 -> 2 using conditional logic, and leaves tier unchanged when tier is already 2

tierLabel returns the correct String for each of the three tier values via branching; describe returns a String exactly of the form "<name> | <tierLabel> | <points> pts | renew=<autoRenew>" using return (not System.out.print) and invoking tierLabel for the label

test_cases.description: default constructor, earn points, upgrade once
full constructor and tierLabel
upgradeTier capped at platinum

prompt:
A role-playing game tracks each playable character with a GameCharacter class. Each character has a name, a class type (such as "Mage" or "Knight"), a current level, and an experience-point total. The class supports the following table of operations.

Method / Constructor | Description
---------------------|------------
public GameCharacter(String n, String c, int lv, int exp) | Constructs a GameCharacter with the given name, class type, level, and experience.
public GameCharacter(String n, String c) | Constructs a GameCharacter with the given name and class type. The level defaults to 1 and the experience defaults to 0.
public void gainExp(int amount) | Adds amount to the character's experience-point total. Multiple calls accumulate.
public String getStatus() | Returns a formatted status string of the form: NAME the CLASSTYPE (LABEL) - Level LV, XP: EXP where LABEL is the literal string "Beginner" if the character's level is less than 5, or "Veteran" if the level is greater than or equal to 5.

Write the complete GameCharacter class. Your implementation must include the class header, all private instance variables, both constructors, and both methods listed above. All instance variables must be private.

parts.label: (complete class)
parts.prompt: Write the entire GameCharacter class as specified, including all private instance variables, both constructors, the gainExp method, and the getStatus method.

rubric.criterion: Class is declared as public class GameCharacter with the exact specified name.
All four instance variables are declared private with correct types: a String for name, a String for class type, an int for level, and an int for experience. No public or static fields.
Both constructors are present with the correct name GameCharacter and parameter lists exactly matching the specification: one (String, String, int, int) and one (String, String).

The four-parameter constructor assigns each of the four fields from its corresponding parameter. The two-parameter constructor assigns name and class type from parameters AND explicitly defaults level to 1 and experience to 0.
Method gainExp is declared public with return type void and a single int parameter.
Method body increments the experience field by the parameter using accumulation (for example experience += amount), not overwriting assignment.

rubric.point_label: getStatus returns correctly formatted string with conditional label
rubric.criterion: Method has return type String, branches on level (< 5 yields "Beginner", otherwise "Veteran"), returns a String of the form NAME the CLASSTYPE (LABEL) - Level LV, XP: EXP, and contains no System.out calls.

test_cases.description: Four-argument constructor with high level produces Veteran label
test_cases.setup_java:
GameCharacter g = new GameCharacter("Aria", "Mage", 7, 1200);
System.out.println(g.getStatus());

test_cases.description: Two-argument constructor defaults level and XP; gainExp accumulates
Boundary: level exactly 5 is Veteran, not Beginner
